styleProfile.summary: Analyzed 51 ARV reference thumbnails: predominantly dark, high-contrast, dominant compositions dark-background, center-heavy, title-band. Average brightness 0.21, contrast 0.82. Recurring motifs: signal / transmission.
typographyNotes: clean lower third reserved for footer
negativeStyleRules: no readable AI-generated text in the background
no celebrity likeness or copyrighted characters

foundryIqMemory.query: Welche kuratierten ARV-Style-Regeln, Paletten und Titelmuster gelten fuer ein YouTube-Livestream-Thumbnail von Audioreworkvisions / Techno Transmissions? Welche Negative Rules und verbotenen Stilbrueche muessen fuer ein Techno-Thumbnail beachtet werden? Welche Prompt-DNA-Fragmente und Layout-Muster passen zu einem 16:9 Techno-Thumbnail mit lokal gerendertem Titeltext?
styleRules: Dark or uncluttered stage; dominant composition with center-heavy subject and dark background
Strong first-frame readability, designed for thumbnail clarity; controlled glow, never glossy
Analog texture: dust, scanlines, hard contrast, matte bloom, paper grain, CRT bleed
Title-band layout: local, readable typography overlay (not generated), amber/white on dark field
patterns: Vertical or centered silhouette with halo or scanner band; title bar overlays at top/bottom
One dominant event or visual motif (signal, frequency, broadcast artifact, techno ritual)
Palette anchors: red, black, deep-blue, amber, grey, magenta, off-white, green from ARV references
Nocturne Signal palette: velvet black, magenta bleed, cyan halo, amber accents, chalk white type
dramaturgy: Formgebung aus dem Nichts: Figure or symbol emerges from darkness and scanlines
Signal findet Lock: Broadcast or gesture stabilizes, revealing the subject for one ritual action
Rave Archaeology: Thumbnail treated as recovered artifact, broadcast relic, or mural fragment
Narrative: Visual prompt acts as transmission – not generic cyberpunk, but underground ritual
continuity: Always use ARV Thumbnail Studio for layout and local typing
Topline: TECHNO TRANSMISSIONS (overlay, clear font, local render)
Slogan overlay: PEACE LOVE TECHNO (must feature, no commercial branding drift)
forbidden: No strobe lights, rapid blinking, harsh flashing, hectic pacing, chaotic glitch spam
No crowded club crowd, violent flicker, frantic glitch, or multiple simultaneous chaotic events
No glossy corporate ad, generic neon cyberpunk city, busy HUD, sterile 3D polish, washed-out beige
No watermark artifacts, unreadable text clutter, generated paragraphs, large fake logos

citations.excerpt: Absolute Avoid: no strobe lights, rapid blinking, harsh flashing, hectic pacing... Style Avoid: no generic corporate motion graphics, sterile stock-video realism, over-clean 3D polish... Text Avoid: no prominent text overlays unless user asks... Composition Avoid: no cluttered backgrounds.

Audioreworkvisions / Hyroglyphs is an audiovisual techno world where visual prompts behave like transmissions, rituals... Must Keep: dark stage, dominant mechanism, strong readability, analog texture, techno as frequency, ritual, archive, signal... Canonical ARV Concepts: Signal transmission, frequency as reality, visual ritual, PEACE LOVE TECHNO.

Primary Palette Families: Nocturne Signal (velvet black, cyan halo, magenta edge bleed, low red signal glow...), Amber Xerox Ritual (black paper void, sunburnt amber...), Dark Sci-Fi Transmission (black/charcoal, cyan rim light, emergency red…).

Opening Fragments: A black electro-boogie silhouette appears like a damaged photocopy on black paper... Texture: toner dust, paper fibers, CRT bleed... Negative Fragment: dark uncluttered background, no strobe lights, no rapid blinking, controlled motion. Avoid prominent text overlays, obvious logos, watermark artifacts, aggressive flashing, glossy ad polish.

Preserve: composition logic, main subject, color-temp, texture family, motion density, one dominant event, OBS readability... Do not destroy: silhouette clarity, style family, loop grammar, ARV identity... Choose 1 to 3 anchor traits per scene, do not stack every motif.

concept.description: Raw funky techno pressure with cosmic rave energy. Analog signal aesthetics, Xerox textures and machine-driven groove collide in a dark broadcast ritual. Audioreworkvisions transmits a hackathon demo as an underground system test between body, frequency and rhythm. PEACE LOVE TECHNO
concept.layout: Title-only banner; flat dark band with centered brutal industrial typography, minimal background.
concept.backgroundPrompt:
Funky Techno broadcast scene, rave, cosmic mood, theme: COSMIC FUNK TRANSMISSION — UNDERGROUND SIGNALS, MACHINE GROOVE, RAVE SYSTEMS. Visual motifs: waveform/signal background, xerox grain, scanlines, analog broadcast residue. Foundry IQ patterns: Vertical or centered silhouette with halo or scanner band; title bar overlays at top/bottom; One dominant event or visual motif (signal, frequency, broadcast artifact, techno ritual); Palette anchors: red, black, deep-blue, amber, grey, magenta, off-white, green from ARV references.
- 16:9 YouTube thumbnail background
- no readable text
- leave clear space for title overlay
- dark analog techno transmission aesthetic
- damaged photocopy / black paper texture
- cyan / amber / magenta accents
- high contrast
- readable composition
- no logos
- no watermark
- no celebrity likeness
- no copyrighted characters
- no generated words

creativeDecision.whyTitle: The user-provided title "AGENTS LEAGUE HACKATHON DEMO" was kept and normalized to uppercase ARV style.
creativeDecision.whyTheme: Theme "COSMIC FUNK TRANSMISSION — UNDERGROUND SIGNALS, MACHINE GROOVE, RAVE SYSTEMS" fits the Funky Techno vibe and the rave/cosmic mood.
creativeDecision.whyLayout: Layout: Title-only banner; flat dark band with centered brutal industrial typography, minimal background.
creativeDecision.whyPalette: Palette red, black, deep-blue, amber, grey, magenta was derived from the analyzed ARV reference profile and Foundry IQ palette guidance.
creativeDecision.whyLocalText: The exact title is rendered locally via Sharp + SVG because AI image generators frequently misspell text; the background only provides scene and posterspace.
